symptoms: Recurrent episodes of binge eating followed by purging behaviors

symptoms: Recurrent episodes of binge eating without compensatory behaviors

diseases.medicine: nutritional counseling, medications (in some cases), support groups

diseases.name: Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder (ARFID)
symptoms: Avoidance or restriction of certain foods or entire food groups
significant weight loss or failure to gain weight
diseases.medicine: psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or family-based therapy), medical monitoring, exposure therapy, occupational therapy

diseases.name: Other Specified Feeding or Eating Disorder (OSFED)
symptoms: Symptoms that do not meet the full criteria for a specific eating disorder
diseases.curing: Treatment is based on the specific symptoms and can include a combination of psychotherapy
diseases.medicine: nutritional counseling, medical monitoring, and medications as necessary

diseases.name: Orthorexia Nervosa
diseases.curing: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or acceptance and commitment therapy)

diseases.name: Rumination Disorder
symptoms: Repeated regurgitation and re-chewing of food

diseases.name: Gestational Diabetes
symptoms: High blood sugar levels during pregnancy
diseases.medicine: healthy diet modifications, regular exercise, insulin injections (if needed), close monitoring by healthcare provider

diseases.name: Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension
diseases.medicine: lifestyle modifications (such as salt restriction, regular exercise), medication (such as antihypertensive drugs) if necessary, close monitoring by healthcare provider

diseases.name: Gestational Hypertension
diseases.medicine: lifestyle modifications (such as salt restriction, regular exercise), medication (such as antihypertensive drugs) if necessary, close monitoring by healthcare provider

diseases.name: Preeclampsia
diseases.medicine: blood pressure monitoring, frequent prenatal visits, medication (such as antihypertensive drugs), magnesium sulfate (for severe cases), delivery of the baby (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Hyperemesis Gravidarum
symptoms: Severe nausea and vomiting during pregnancy
diseases.medicine: anti-nausea medications, nutritional support, rest, monitoring for weight loss and electrolyte imbalance

diseases.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
diseases.medicine: increased fluid intake, maintaining good hygiene, urine culture and sensitivity testing to guide antibiotic selection

diseases.name: Anemia
diseases.medicine: dietary changes (iron-rich foods), managing underlying causes (such as treating iron deficiency or vitamin B12 deficiency), prenatal vitamins

diseases.name: Pregnancy-Related Back Pain
diseases.curing: Prenatal exercises (such as stretching and strengthening exercises)
diseases.medicine: good posture, heat or cold therapy, prenatal massages, supportive devices (such as pregnancy belts), physical therapy

diseases.name: Round Ligament Pain
symptoms: Sharp or aching pain in the lower abdomen or groin
diseases.medicine: changing positions slowly, warm compresses, gentle stretching, avoiding sudden movements

diseases.name: Placenta Previa
symptoms: painless bleeding in the third trimester
diseases.medicine: avoiding strenuous activity, frequent prenatal check-ups, potential hospitalization, potential cesarean delivery

diseases.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
symptoms: and pain in the hand and fingers
diseases.medicine: avoiding repetitive hand movements, applying cold or hot compresses, gentle exercises, ergonomic adjustments

diseases.name: Sciatica
symptoms: or numbness that radiates from the lower back through the hips and down the leg
diseases.medicine: gentle stretching exercises, warm or cold compresses, prenatal massages, maintaining good posture, avoiding activities that worsen the pain

diseases.name: Varicose Veins
diseases.medicine: wearing compression stockings, regular exercise, avoiding prolonged sitting or standing, maintaining a healthy weight

diseases.name: Hemorrhoids
symptoms: or painful veins in the rectal area
diseases.medicine: staying hydrated, avoiding straining during bowel movements, warm sitz baths, topical creams or ointments, over-the-counter pain relievers (after consulting with a healthcare provider)

diseases.name: Gestational Cholestasis
symptoms: particularly on the hands and feet
diseases.medicine: nan

diseases.name: Liver Cancer
symptoms: jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes)
diseases.medicine: Radiation therapy, Chemotherapy, Targeted therapy, Immunotherapy, Radiofrequency ablation, Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE), Selective internal radiation therapy (SIRT), Palliative care, Clinical trials

diseases.name: Atelectasis
diseases.curing: Treatment of the underlying cause (such as removal of airway obstruction
diseases.medicine: treatment of lung infection), deep breathing exercises, coughing techniques, incentive spirometry, chest physiotherapy, bronchodilator medications, supplemental oxygen therapy

diseases.name: Ectopic Pregnancy
symptoms: shoulder pain (if the fallopian tube ruptures)
diseases.medicine: potential medical procedures (such as medication or surgery to remove the ectopic pregnancy), close monitoring, emotional support

diseases.name: Choledocholithiasis
symptoms: Abdominal pain (often in the upper right quadrant)
endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) with stone removal
diseases.curing: Non-surgical treatment options: Medications to manage pain and discomfort
diseases.medicine: antibiotics

diseases.name: Cirrhosis
diseases.medicine: slow down disease progression, and address underlying causes. It may include: lifestyle modifications (e.g., avoiding alcohol, maintaining a healthy diet), medication (e.g., diuretics, lactulose, antiviral drugs), procedures (e.g., paracentesis, transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt), liver transplantation (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm
diseases.curing: Regular monitoring and imaging tests to assess aneurysm size and growth
diseases.medicine: lifestyle modifications (e.g., blood pressure control, avoiding smoking, managing cholesterol levels), medication (e.g., beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors) to manage blood pressure and reduce the risk of rupture, surgical intervention

diseases.name: Subdural hemorrhage
diseases.medicine: close monitoring of vital signs and neurological status, diagnostic imaging (such as CT or MRI scan), potential surgical intervention (such as craniotomy or burr hole evacuation), medication to manage symptoms

diseases.name: Congenital rubella
diseases.curing: Supportive care to manage symptoms and complications
diseases.medicine: early intervention services for developmental delays, educational support, vision and hearing interventions, specialized medical care as needed

diseases.name: Diabetic retinopathy
symptoms: dark or empty areas in the visual field
diseases.medicine: blood pressure control, cholesterol management, regular eye examinations, laser treatment, intravitreal injections of anti-VEGF medications, vitrectomy

diseases.name: Fibromyalgia
diseases.curing: Multidisciplinary approach: Medications for pain management (such as analgesics
diseases.medicine: antidepressants, and anticonvulsants), physical therapy, occupational therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, relaxation techniques, stress management, exercise programs

diseases.name: Ischemia of the Bowel
diseases.medicine: intravenous fluids and medications to stabilize the patient, bowel rest, resection, bypass procedure, potential angioplasty

diseases.name: Fetal Alcohol Syndrome
diseases.curing: Early intervention services for developmental delays
diseases.medicine: educational support, speech and occupational therapy, behavioral interventions, medical management of associated health issues

diseases.name: Peritonitis
diseases.curing: Intravenous antibiotics to treat the infection
diseases.medicine: intravenous fluids to restore hydration, drainage or removal of any underlying fluid or abscess, surgery to repair or remove the source of infection

diseases.name: Abdominal Injury
diseases.medicine: X-ray, CT scan, ultrasound, stabilization of vital signs, pain management, observation for signs of internal bleeding, potential surgical intervention, wound care

diseases.name: Acute Pancreatitis
diseases.medicine: intravenous fluids to restore hydration, pain management, bowel rest, nutritional support, medications to control pain and inflammation

diseases.name: Thrombophlebitis
diseases.curing: Medications to manage pain and inflammation
diseases.medicine: compression stockings or bandages to promote blood flow, elevation of the affected limb, warm compresses, regular movement and exercise, treatment of underlying causes (such as infection or intravenous catheter removal), anticoagulant therapy to prevent clot formation or progression

diseases.name: Asthma
symptoms: especially at night or in response to triggers such as allergens or exercise
diseases.curing: Long-term control medications (such as inhaled corticosteroids
diseases.medicine: long-acting beta-agonists), quick-relief medications (such as short-acting beta-agonists), avoidance of triggers (such as allergens, smoke), regular monitoring of symptoms and lung function, development of an asthma action plan, proper inhaler technique, regular follow-up with a healthcare provider, emergency medications (such as oral corticosteroids) as needed

diseases.name: Restless Leg Syndrome
symptoms: usually in the evening or at night
urge to move legs for relief
diseases.medicine: avoiding triggers like caffeine), good sleep hygiene (maintaining a regular sleep schedule), leg massages or stretching exercises, hot or cold packs, medication therapy (such as dopamine agonists, anticonvulsants, opioids), iron supplementation (if iron deficiency is present)

diseases.name: Emphysema
diseases.medicine: medications to manage symptoms (such as bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids), pulmonary rehabilitation (exercise training, breathing techniques), supplemental oxygen therapy (in advanced cases), vaccination against respiratory infections, avoiding exposure to irritants (such as secondhand smoke or air pollutants), regular monitoring of symptoms and lung function, surgical interventions (such as lung volume reduction surgery or lung transplantation)

diseases.name: Induced Abortion
diseases.curing: Medical Abortion: Medication (such as mifepristone and misoprostol) is used to induce abortion
diseases.medicine: which can be done in the early stages of pregnancy. Surgical Abortion: A healthcare professional performs a procedure to remove the pregnancy, which can involve suction aspiration or dilation and curettage (D&C). It's important to consult with a healthcare professional to discuss the options and determine the most suitable method.

diseases.name: Acute Sinusitis
diseases.curing: Home remedies (such as nasal saline irrigation
diseases.medicine: steam inhalation), over-the-counter pain relievers (such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen), nasal decongestants (for short-term use), nasal corticosteroids (to reduce inflammation), plenty of fluids and rest, warm compresses, avoidance of irritants (such as smoke or allergens), prescription antibiotics (if bacterial infection is suspected or confirmed), management of underlying causes (such as allergies or nasal polyps), symptom relief medications (such as cough suppressants or antihistamines)

diseases.name: Von Hippel-Lindau Disease
symptoms: retinal angiomas (abnormal blood vessel growth in the retina)
diseases.curing: Regular monitoring and surveillance to detect and manage tumor growth and associated complications
diseases.medicine: surgical removal or treatment of tumors if necessary, embolization or radiation therapy for certain tumors, medication or surgery to manage hormonal issues (such as pheochromocytomas), genetic counseling and testing, screening and management for other potential complications (such as kidney function monitoring)

symptoms: inability to control or limit alcohol consumption
sweating) when attempting to stop or reduce alcohol use
continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems

diseases.name: Opioid Use Disorder
symptoms: strong desire or cravings for opioids
inability to control or reduce opioid use
anxiety) when attempting to stop or reduce use
continued use despite negative consequences (such as legal issues
diseases.medicine: buprenorphine, naltrexone), counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, contingency management), support groups (such as Narcotics Anonymous), harm reduction strategies, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, ongoing monitoring and support

diseases.name: Stimulant Use Disorder
symptoms: increased heart rate and blood pressure
diseases.curing: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy
diseases.medicine: motivational interviewing), support groups, medication (if applicable), addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

diseases.name: Cannabis Use Disorder

continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems
diseases.curing: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy

diseases.name: Benzodiazepine Use Disorder
symptoms: tremors) when attempting to stop or reduce benzodiazepine use
continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems
diseases.curing: Gradual tapering off benzodiazepines under medical supervision
diseases.medicine: counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

diseases.name: Hallucinogen Use Disorder
diseases.curing: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy
diseases.medicine: motivational interviewing), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

diseases.name: Sedative, Hypnotic, or Anxiolytic Use Disorder
symptoms: tremors) when attempting to stop or reduce use
continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems
diseases.medicine: hypnotics, or anxiolytics under medical supervision, counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

diseases.name: Postpartum Depression
symptoms: loss of interest or pleasure in activities
changes in appetite or sleep patterns
thoughts of self-harm or harming the baby
diseases.curing: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or interpersonal therapy)
diseases.medicine: support groups or peer counseling, antidepressant medication (if recommended by a healthcare professional), hormone therapy (in some cases), lifestyle changes (such as exercise, healthy diet, sleep hygiene), involving a partner or family in the care of the baby, social support and understanding from family and friends, regular follow-up and monitoring by healthcare professionals

diseases.name: Coronary Atherosclerosis
symptoms: jaw or arm pain (in some cases)
heart attack (when a coronary artery becomes completely blocked)
diseases.curing: Lifestyle changes (such as a heart-healthy diet
diseases.medicine: regular exercise, weight management, smoking cessation), medication (such as aspirin, cholesterol-lowering medications, beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, nitroglycerin), procedures (such as angioplasty and stenting, coronary artery bypass grafting), cardiac rehabilitation, management of other risk factors (such as high blood pressure, diabetes)

diseases.name: Spondylitis
symptoms: especially in the morning or after periods of inactivity
limited range of motion in the spine
inflammation and pain in other joints (such as hips
discomfort or pain in the neck
tenderness or swelling in affected areas
tingling or numbness in extremities in severe cases
diseases.medicine: corticosteroids, disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs), physical therapy (including stretching and strengthening exercises), heat or cold therapy, posture correction techniques, lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, weight management), assistive devices (such as braces or supports), surgical intervention (in rare cases), pain management techniques (such as acupuncture or transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation)

diseases.name: Pituitary Adenoma
symptoms: vision problems (such as blurred vision or loss of peripheral vision)
hormonal imbalances (such as excessive production or deficiency of specific hormones)
diseases.medicine: non-secreting tumors that are not causing significant symptoms), medication (to manage hormonal imbalances or reduce tumor size), surgery (transsphenoidal surgery to remove the tumor), radiation therapy (in cases where surgery is not possible or to treat residual or recurrent tumors), targeted therapy (in cases of aggressive or resistant tumors), hormone replacement therapy (if necessary), regular follow-up and monitoring to assess tumor growth and hormone levels, management of associated complications or hormone deficiencies

diseases.name: Uterine Fibroids
symptoms: infertility or recurrent miscarriages (in some cases)
diseases.curing: Watchful waiting (for small or asymptomatic fibroids)
diseases.medicine: medication (such as hormonal birth control, GnRH agonists, progestin-releasing intrauterine devices), minimally invasive procedures (such as uterine artery embolization, myomectomy), hysteroscopic procedures (such as hysteroscopic myomectomy or endometrial ablation), surgical removal of fibroids (such as laparoscopic or robotic-assisted myomectomy, hysterectomy), focused ultrasound ablation, hormonal therapy, alternative approaches (such as acupuncture or herbal remedies)

diseases.name: Idiopathic Nonmenstrual Bleeding
symptoms: Abnormal vaginal bleeding outside of the menstrual period
spotting or light bleeding between periods
diseases.curing: Evaluation to rule out underlying causes (such as hormonal imbalances
diseases.medicine: polyps, infections, or uterine abnormalities), hormonal therapy (such as birth control pills, hormone replacement therapy), non-hormonal medications (such as tranexamic acid), dilation and curettage (if necessary to remove excessive tissue or investigate the cause), endometrial ablation (in some cases), hysteroscopy (to examine the uterus), surgery (in rare cases), counseling or support for emotional well-being

diseases.name: Chalazion
symptoms: red lump or swelling on the eyelid
blurred vision (if the chalazion affects the line of sight)
diseases.curing: Warm compresses (to help soften and promote drainage of the blocked oil gland)
diseases.medicine: gentle massage of the affected area, eyelid hygiene (keeping the area clean), over-the-counter lubricating eye drops, topical antibiotic ointments (if there is a risk of infection), corticosteroid injections (for larger or persistent chalazia), surgical drainage or excision (in rare cases or if conservative treatments are ineffective)

diseases.name: Ovarian Torsion
symptoms: Sudden and severe abdominal or pelvic pain on one side
diseases.curing: Emergency medical attention (as ovarian torsion is a medical emergency)
diseases.medicine: imaging tests (such as ultrasound or CT scan) to diagnose torsion, surgical intervention (laparoscopic or open surgery) to untwist the ovary and restore blood flow, removal of the ovary (if it is damaged or non-viable), pain management (medications for pain relief)

diseases.name: Vaginal Yeast Infection
symptoms: Itching and irritation in the vagina and vulva
redness and swelling of the vulva
rash or soreness in the affected area
diseases.medicine: ointments, or suppositories (such as miconazole, clotrimazole, or terconazole), prescription-strength antifungal medications (in severe or recurrent cases), oral antifungal medications (if recommended by a healthcare professional), topical corticosteroids (for relief of itching and inflammation), maintaining good hygiene practices, avoiding irritants (such as scented products), wearing breathable cotton underwear, avoiding tight-fitting clothing, probiotics (to restore healthy vaginal flora)

diseases.name: Mastoiditis
symptoms: swelling and redness of the ear or the area behind the ear
drainage of fluid or pus from the ear
tenderness or pain when touching the area
diseases.curing: Antibiotics (oral or intravenous) to treat the underlying bacterial infection
diseases.medicine: pain relievers (such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen) for pain and fever, ear drops (if there is drainage from the ear), surgical intervention (in severe or chronic cases), such as mastoidectomy (removal of infected mastoid bone cells) or tympanostomy tube placement (to relieve pressure and drain fluid from the middle ear), supportive care (rest, hydration, warm compresses)

diseases.name: Lung Contusion
symptoms: difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
coughing up blood or pink-tinged sputum
diseases.medicine: pain management, and respiratory support), monitoring in a hospital setting (to assess lung function and overall stability), treatment of associated injuries (such as rib fractures), management of complications (such as pneumonia or respiratory failure), prevention of further complications (such as deep vein thrombosis or pressure ulcers), respiratory therapy (including breathing exercises and airway clearance techniques), possible use of mechanical ventilation in severe cases

diseases.name: Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy (HOCM)
symptoms: especially during physical activity or exertion
diseases.curing: Medications to relieve symptoms and improve heart function (such as beta-blockers
diseases.medicine: calcium channel blockers, and disopyramide), medications to prevent abnormal heart rhythms (such as anti-arrhythmic drugs), medications to reduce blood clotting (such as aspirin or anticoagulants), implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) placement (to monitor and correct abnormal heart rhythms), septal myectomy (surgical removal of a portion of the thickened heart muscle), alcohol septal ablation (destruction of excess heart muscle with alcohol injection), lifestyle modifications (such as avoiding strenuous activities, managing stress, and maintaining a healthy weight), regular follow-up visits with a cardiologist

diseases.name: Pulmonary Eosinophilia
symptoms: elevated eosinophil count in the blood or sputum
diseases.curing: Identification and treatment of underlying causes (such as parasitic infections
diseases.medicine: drug reactions, or allergic reactions), corticosteroids (such as prednisone) to reduce inflammation, bronchodilators (such as inhalers) to relieve bronchospasm and improve airflow, antiparasitic medications (if parasitic infection is identified), immunosuppressive medications (in severe cases), avoidance of triggers or allergens (if identified), symptomatic treatment for associated symptoms (such as fever or cough), regular monitoring and follow-up with a healthcare professional

diseases.name: Corneal Disorder
symptoms: redness or irritation of the eye
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the specific corneal disorder and may include: artificial tears or lubricating eye drops
diseases.medicine: antibiotic or antifungal eye drops (if infection is present), corticosteroid eye drops or ointments (to reduce inflammation), bandage contact lenses, therapeutic contact lenses, corneal transplantation (in severe cases), refractive surgeries (such as LASIK or PRK) to correct vision, protective eyewear or sunglasses, avoiding irritants or triggers, regular eye examinations and monitoring, lifestyle modifications (such as proper eye hygiene and avoiding eye strain)

diseases.name: Foreign Body in the Gastrointestinal Tract
diseases.medicine: smooth, and non-sharp objects that are likely to pass through the digestive tract without complications), endoscopic removal (using a flexible tube with a camera to locate and remove the foreign body), surgical intervention (in cases of severe complications, large or sharp objects, or failed endoscopic removal attempts), supportive care (such as intravenous fluids, pain management, and antibiotics if infection is present), imaging studies (such as X-rays or CT scans) to evaluate the location and extent of the foreign body, consultation with a gastroenterologist or surgeon for appropriate management

diseases.name: Endophthalmitis
symptoms: floaters (dark spots or cobwebs in the vision)
diseases.curing: Immediate medical attention is required. Treatment may include: intravenous antibiotics or antifungal medications
diseases.medicine: intravitreal injections (injections of antibiotics or antifungal agents directly into the eye), vitrectomy (surgical removal of the gel-like fluid within the eye), topical or oral medications (to manage pain, inflammation, or infection), frequent follow-up visits with an ophthalmologist, supportive care (such as warm compresses or lubricating eye drops)

diseases.name: Achalasia
diseases.medicine: surgery, medication

diseases.name: Conductive Hearing Loss
diseases.medicine: surgical intervention, hearing aids

diseases.name: Abdominal Hernia
diseases.medicine: hernia truss, surgical repair, lifestyle changes

diseases.name: Marijuana Abuse
diseases.medicine: counseling, support groups, medication, detoxification

diseases.medicine: Amphotericin B, Fluconazole), supportive care

diseases.name: Bursitis

diseases.name: Actinic Keratosis

diseases.name: Acute Otitis Media

diseases.name: Astigmatism
diseases.medicine: contact lenses, refractive surgery (LASIK, PRK), orthokeratology, toric intraocular lenses

diseases.medicine: behavioral, etc.), surgery (for tumor removal), therapy (physical, occupational, speech), educational interventions, support services, genetic counseling, monitoring of organ involvement

diseases.name: Empyema
symptoms: productive cough with foul-smelling or bloody sputum
diseases.medicine: drainage (chest tube insertion, thoracentesis), surgical intervention (decortication), supportive care, treatment of underlying cause

diseases.name: Presbycusis
diseases.medicine: assistive listening devices, cochlear implants (in severe cases), communication strategies, counseling, regular hearing evaluations

diseases.name: Neonatal Jaundice
symptoms: Yellowing of the skin and eyes
diseases.medicine: exchange transfusion (in severe cases), monitoring of bilirubin levels, addressing underlying causes (e.g., breastfeeding)

diseases.name: Dislocation of the Elbow
symptoms: numbness or tingling in the hand/arm
diseases.curing: Closed reduction (realigning the bones without surgery)
diseases.medicine: immobilization (splint or cast), physical therapy, pain management, surgical intervention (in complex or recurrent cases)

diseases.name: Spondylosis
symptoms: numbness or weakness in the extremities
diseases.medicine: muscle relaxants), physical therapy, exercise, posture improvement, heat or cold therapy, spinal injections, surgery (in severe cases or if conservative treatments fail)

diseases.name: Herpangina
symptoms: painful sores or blisters in the mouth and throat
diseases.medicine: throat lozenges, mouth rinses), rest, fluids, maintaining good oral hygiene, avoiding spicy or acidic foods

diseases.name: Poisoning due to Antidepressants
diseases.curing: Seek immediate medical assistance or contact a poison control center
diseases.medicine: activated charcoal (if indicated), supportive care (maintaining vital signs, airway management), gastric lavage (in certain cases), administration of antidotes (if available and appropriate), monitoring and management of complications

diseases.name: Infection of Open Wound
symptoms: pain or tenderness at the wound site
pus or drainage from the wound
diseases.curing: Cleaning and irrigation of the wound
diseases.medicine: antibiotic therapy (oral or intravenous), wound dressings, wound debridement (if necessary), tetanus prophylaxis

diseases.name: Protein Deficiency
diseases.curing: Increasing protein intake through diet (lean meats
diseases.medicine: fish, dairy, legumes, nuts), protein supplements, nutritional counseling, addressing underlying causes (e.g., malabsorption)

diseases.name: Myoclonus
diseases.medicine: medication adjustments, metabolic imbalances), medications (anti-seizure drugs, tranquilizers), physical therapy, supportive care

diseases.name: Bone Spur of the Calcaneus
diseases.medicine: orthotic devices, physical therapy, stretching exercises, shoe modifications, corticosteroid injections, surgical removal (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Von Willebrand Disease
symptoms: prolonged or excessive bleeding from cuts or injuries
diseases.medicine: replacement therapy with von Willebrand factor (VWF) concentrate or cryoprecipitate, oral contraceptives (for women with heavy menstrual bleeding), avoiding certain medications, dental care precautions, regular medical follow-up

diseases.name: Heart Block
diseases.medicine: calcium channel blockers), pacemaker implantation (in severe cases or symptomatic heart block), underlying condition management

diseases.name: Colonic Polyp
symptoms: Most colonic polyps do not cause symptoms. Some larger polyps or certain types may cause rectal bleeding or changes in bowel habits
diseases.medicine: surveillance colonoscopy (regular monitoring for recurrence or new polyps), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, smoking cessation), follow-up based on pathology findings

diseases.name: Hypospadias
symptoms: Abnormal positioning of the urethral opening on the underside of the penis
diseases.medicine: hormone therapy (in some cases), counseling and support for the child and family

diseases.name: Magnesium Deficiency
diseases.medicine: oral magnesium supplements, intravenous magnesium therapy (in severe cases), addressing underlying causes (e.g., malabsorption), medical supervision and monitoring

diseases.name: Female Infertility of Unknown Cause
symptoms: Inability to conceive after one year of unprotected intercourse
diseases.medicine: imaging, genetic screening), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, stress reduction), fertility treatments (assisted reproductive technologies, fertility medications)

diseases.name: Pericarditis
symptoms: worsens with deep breaths or lying down
diseases.curing: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) to reduce inflammation and pain
diseases.medicine: colchicine (for recurrent or persistent pericarditis), corticosteroids (in severe cases or when NSAIDs are ineffective), antibiotics (if bacterial infection is present), lifestyle modifications (rest, avoiding strenuous activity), treating underlying causes (if identified), potential pericardiocentesis (fluid drainage)

diseases.name: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
diseases.medicine: parent training programs, medication (such as stimulants or non-stimulants), individualized education plans (IEPs) for academic support, accommodations and modifications in school, psychoeducation, counseling

diseases.name: Neuromyelitis Optica
symptoms: loss of bladder or bowel control)
diseases.medicine: plasma exchange (to remove antibodies from the blood), immunosuppressive therapy (such as azathioprine or rituximab), supportive care to manage symptoms, physical therapy or rehabilitation for functional recovery

diseases.name: Pulmonic Valve Disease
diseases.medicine: beta-blockers, or calcium channel blockers), surgical interventions (such as balloon valvuloplasty or valve replacement), lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, heart-healthy diet, smoking cessation), regular follow-up with a cardiologist

diseases.name: Tietze Syndrome
symptoms: swelling and tenderness of the cartilage connecting the ribs to the breastbone
diseases.medicine: pain relievers, local heat or ice application, avoiding activities that worsen symptoms, supportive measures (such as wearing loose clothing or using supportive pillows), physical therapy (to improve posture and strengthen muscles), corticosteroid injections (in some cases), stress reduction techniques

diseases.name: Cranial Nerve Palsy
symptoms: Depends on the specific cranial nerve involved; symptoms can include vision changes
loss of sensation in the face or other areas
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and specific cranial nerve affected. It may include medications (such as corticosteroids or antiviral drugs)
diseases.medicine: physical therapy, speech therapy, occupational therapy, eye exercises, surgery (in some cases), and management of any underlying conditions.

diseases.name: Conversion Disorder
symptoms: Neurological symptoms that cannot be explained by a medical condition or injury
diseases.curing: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or psychodynamic therapy)
diseases.medicine: physical therapy or rehabilitation (to improve physical function and reduce disability), addressing underlying stressors or psychological factors, supportive care and reassurance, potentially medications (such as antidepressants or anti-anxiety medications)

diseases.name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome
symptoms: changes in skin color or temperature
diseases.medicine: stretching, and mirror therapy), pain management techniques (such as medications, nerve blocks, or spinal cord stimulation), sympathetic nerve blocks, psychological support (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or counseling), occupational therapy, graded motor imagery, transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS), patient education and self-management techniques

diseases.name: Otosclerosis
diseases.medicine: surgical interventions (such as stapedectomy or stapedotomy to replace the affected bone in the middle ear), sodium fluoride therapy (to slow down the progression of the condition), communication strategies and support (such as lip-reading or sign language), regular hearing assessments

diseases.name: Hypothyroidism
diseases.curing: Hormone replacement therapy with synthetic thyroid hormone (levothyroxine)
diseases.medicine: regular monitoring of thyroid hormone levels and adjustment of medication dosage, lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet and regular exercise), management of associated symptoms (such as cholesterol-lowering medications or antidepressants), education and support for long-term management

diseases.name: Primary Insomnia
symptoms: Difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep
diseases.medicine: sleep hygiene practices (such as maintaining a regular sleep schedule, creating a comfortable sleep environment), relaxation techniques, stimulus control techniques (such as limiting bed activities to sleep only), sleep restriction therapy, medications for insomnia (such as sedative-hypnotics, melatonin agonists)

diseases.name: Lice
symptoms: Itching and visible presence of lice or nits (eggs) in the hair
diseases.curing: Over-the-counter or prescription medications specifically designed to kill lice (such as pediculicides)
diseases.medicine: manual removal of lice and nits using a fine-toothed comb, washing and drying of infested clothing and bedding at high temperatures, vacuuming of upholstery and household surfaces, disinfection of personal items that cannot be washed or dried, avoiding direct head-to-head contact with infested individuals, education and prevention strategies

diseases.name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
symptoms: tingling or numbness in the hands and feet
diseases.curing: Vitamin B12 supplementation (such as oral supplements or injections)
diseases.medicine: dietary changes to include B12-rich foods (such as meat, fish, eggs, and dairy products), identification and management of underlying causes (such as pernicious anemia or malabsorption disorders), management of associated symptoms or complications (such as nerve pain or balance problems), regular monitoring of B12 levels and treatment adjustment

diseases.name: Vulvodynia
symptoms: Persistent pain or discomfort in the vulva
diseases.curing: Topical medications (such as lidocaine or estrogen creams)
diseases.medicine: nerve blocks, physical therapy (including pelvic floor muscle relaxation exercises), cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), biofeedback, medications (such as tricyclic antidepressants or anticonvulsants), vulvar care and hygiene practices, stress management techniques, alternative therapies (such as acupuncture or herbal remedies)

diseases.name: Endometriosis
diseases.curing: Pain management (such as nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs [NSAIDs]
diseases.medicine: hormonal contraceptives, or progestins), hormone therapy (such as gonadotropin-releasing hormone [GnRH] agonists or oral contraceptives), surgical interventions (such as laparoscopic excision or hysterectomy), fertility treatments (if pregnancy is desired), lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, stress management), complementary therapies (such as acupuncture or dietary changes)

diseases.name: Vasculitis
symptoms: Inflammation of blood vessels leading to various symptoms depending on the affected organs
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the type and severity of vasculitis. It may include corticosteroids
diseases.medicine: immunosuppressive medications (such as cyclophosphamide or azathioprine), biologic therapies (such as rituximab), disease-specific treatments (such as plasmapheresis for certain types), supportive care to manage symptoms (such as pain relievers or blood pressure medications), monitoring and management of associated complications (such as organ damage or infections)

diseases.name: Concussion
diseases.medicine: gradual return to normal activities, symptom management (such as pain relievers or anti-nausea medication), monitoring for complications (such as changes in neurological function), cognitive rehabilitation (if persistent cognitive problems), management of associated symptoms (such as sleep disturbances or mood changes), education and support for recovery, avoiding further head injuries

diseases.name: Oral Leukoplakia
symptoms: White patches or plaques in the mouth
often on the tongue or inside of the cheeks
diseases.medicine: identification and elimination of potential irritants (such as tobacco or alcohol), improving oral hygiene practices, medication (such as topical retinoids or antifungal agents), surgical removal (in some cases), cryotherapy (freezing the patches), laser therapy, lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet), regular dental check-ups, education and support

diseases.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
symptoms: swelling of the legs or ankles
diseases.curing: Management of underlying conditions (such as diabetes or high blood pressure)
diseases.medicine: lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet, regular exercise, smoking cessation), medications (such as ACE inhibitors or diuretics), dialysis (in advanced stages), kidney transplant (in end-stage kidney disease), management of associated complications (such as anemia or bone disease), regular monitoring and follow-up with a nephrologist, education and support for self-care

diseases.name: Wilson Disease
diseases.curing: Medications to remove copper from the body (chelators)
diseases.medicine: dietary changes, liver transplant (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Vesicoureteral Reflux
diseases.medicine: bladder training exercises, surgical correction (in some cases)

diseases.name: Vitamin A Deficiency
diseases.medicine: dietary changes to include foods rich in vitamin A

diseases.name: Gonorrhea
diseases.curing: Antibiotics (dual therapy with ceftriaxone and azithromycin)
diseases.medicine: safe sexual practices, partner notification

diseases.name: Fracture of the Rib
symptoms: tenderness or swelling over the ribcage
diseases.medicine: rest, immobilization (splinting or bracing), physical therapy

diseases.name: Ependymoma
diseases.medicine: radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, rehabilitation

diseases.name: Hepatitis due to a Toxin
diseases.medicine: removal or avoidance of the toxic substance, management of symptoms

diseases.name: Vaginal Cyst
symptoms: Small lump or swelling in the vaginal wall
diseases.medicine: warm compresses, sitz baths, surgical removal (if necessary)

diseases.name: Chronic Knee Pain
diseases.medicine: physical therapy, lifestyle modifications, knee braces or supports

diseases.name: Pinguecula
symptoms: Yellowish or white growth on the conjunctiva (eye's surface)
diseases.medicine: lubricating eye drops, sunglasses for UV protection, surgical removal (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Hypergammaglobulinemia
symptoms: Elevated levels of gamma globulins in the blood
may be asymptomatic or associated with other underlying conditions
diseases.curing: Treatment of underlying cause or associated condition
diseases.medicine: immunosuppressive therapy (in some cases)

diseases.name: Pituitary Disorder
symptoms: Various symptoms depending on the specific disorder
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the specific pituitary disorder and may involve medications
diseases.medicine: surgery, hormone replacement therapy

diseases.name: Kidney Stone
symptoms: Severe pain in the side or back
diseases.medicine: increased fluid intake, medication to facilitate stone passage, minimally invasive procedures or surgery

diseases.name: Autism
diseases.medicine: educational interventions, speech and language therapy, medications for associated symptoms

diseases.name: Cat Scratch Disease
diseases.medicine: doxycycline), pain management, supportive care

diseases.name: Chronic Glaucoma
diseases.medicine: oral medications, laser therapy, surgery (trabeculectomy, implantable devices), regular eye exams

diseases.name: Retinal Detachment
symptoms: Floaters (spots or cobweb-like shapes in vision)
diseases.medicine: scleral buckle, vitrectomy), laser therapy, cryotherapy, gas or silicone oil injection, positioning, regular eye exams

diseases.name: Aplastic Anemia
diseases.medicine: bone marrow-stimulating medications, immunosuppressive therapy, stem cell transplant (in severe cases), supportive care

diseases.name: Overflow Incontinence
symptoms: Frequent or constant dribbling of urine
inability to completely empty the bladder
diseases.medicine: double voiding, catheterization, medications (alpha-blockers, anticholinergics), surgery (in severe cases), lifestyle modifications

diseases.name: Rabies
diseases.medicine: wound care, administration of rabies vaccine and immunoglobulin, supportive care, monitoring and management of symptoms

diseases.name: Hemolytic Anemia
diseases.medicine: blood transfusions, medications (immunosuppressants, corticosteroids), splenectomy (in some cases), supportive care, folic acid or iron supplementation

diseases.name: Lateral Epicondylitis (Tennis Elbow)
symptoms: Pain or tenderness on the outer side of the elbow
diseases.medicine: ice or cold therapy, compression, elbow brace or strap, pain relievers, physical therapy exercises, corticosteroid injections (in severe cases), ergonomic modifications

diseases.name: Syphilis
symptoms: Primary stage: painless sores (chancre) at the site of infection
tertiary stage: severe complications affecting the heart
diseases.medicine: treatment of complications, regular follow-up and testing

diseases.name: Diabetic Kidney Disease
symptoms: swelling in the legs and ankles
diseases.medicine: blood sugar control, medications to protect the kidneys (such as ACE inhibitors or ARBs), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, quitting smoking), management of underlying diabetes, treatment of complications (such as dialysis or kidney transplantation in advanced stages)

diseases.name: Nose Disorder
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (such as allergies
diseases.medicine: sinusitis, or nasal polyps), and may include medications (such as decongestants, antihistamines, nasal sprays), saline nasal irrigation, steam inhalation, allergy management, antibiotics (if bacterial infection is present), surgical intervention (in some cases)

diseases.name: Drug Withdrawal
diseases.curing: Gradual tapering of the drug under medical supervision
diseases.medicine: medications to manage withdrawal symptoms (such as benzodiazepines, clonidine, or buprenorphine), behavioral therapy, support groups, counseling, rehabilitation programs

diseases.name: Dental Caries
symptoms: visible pits or holes in the teeth
diseases.medicine: crowns, root canal treatment, tooth extraction (in severe cases), oral hygiene practices (brushing and flossing), regular dental check-ups, fluoride treatments, dietary modifications (reducing sugary and acidic foods and drinks), dental sealants (for prevention), education on proper oral care

diseases.name: Hypercholesterolemia
symptoms: High levels of cholesterol in the blood
fatty deposits (xanthomas) on the skin or tendons
diseases.medicine: regular exercise, weight management, smoking cessation), medications to lower cholesterol levels (such as statins), regular monitoring of cholesterol levels, management of underlying conditions (such as diabetes or high blood pressure), potential additional treatments (such as bile acid sequestrants or PCSK9 inhibitors)

diseases.name: Fracture of the Patella
symptoms: difficulty straightening or bending the knee
diseases.medicine: cast, or brace), pain management, physical therapy, exercises to restore mobility and strength, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases or if displacement is present)

diseases.name: Kidney Failure
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and may include medications to manage blood pressure and symptoms
diseases.medicine: dietary modifications (restricting salt, protein, and potassium), dialysis (hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis) or kidney transplantation (in end-stage kidney failure), treatment of complications, management of fluid and electrolyte balance, lifestyle modifications (exercise, quitting smoking, healthy diet)

diseases.name: Fracture of the Neck
symptoms: difficulty moving or turning the head
numbness or weakness in the arms or legs
diseases.medicine: brace), pain management, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Muscle Spasm
diseases.medicine: heat or cold therapy, over-the-counter pain relievers, muscle relaxants, physical therapy, lifestyle modifications

diseases.name: Hemophilia
symptoms: prolonged bleeding from wounds or after dental work or surgery
spontaneous bleeding in joints or muscles
diseases.curing: Replacement therapy with clotting factor concentrates
diseases.medicine: medications to control bleeding, physical therapy, joint protection measures, genetic counseling and testing for family members

diseases.name: Hyperosmotic Hyperketotic State
diseases.curing: Insulin therapy to regulate blood sugar levels
diseases.medicine: intravenous fluids to correct dehydration, electrolyte replacement, correction of underlying causes (e.g., diabetes management)

diseases.name: Peritonsillar Abscess
diseases.medicine: drainage of the abscess (needle aspiration or incision and drainage), pain relievers, warm saltwater gargles, rest, fluids, surgical removal of the tonsils (in chronic or recurrent cases)

diseases.name: Gastroparesis
diseases.medicine: frequent meals; low-fiber, low-fat diet), medication to promote gastric emptying, managing underlying cause if possible

diseases.name: Fat Embolism
symptoms: petechial rash (small red or purple spots)
diseases.medicine: fluid resuscitation), managing symptoms (pain medication, anticoagulants), treatment of underlying cause if possible

diseases.name: Polycythemia Vera
diseases.medicine: medication (hydroxyurea, interferon), low-dose aspirin, managing complications (blood thinners, medication for itching)

diseases.name: Thrombocytopenia
symptoms: petechiae (red or purple spots on the skin)
diseases.medicine: platelet transfusion (if severe), medication (corticosteroids, immunosuppressants), avoiding medications that can worsen platelet counts

diseases.name: Head and Neck Cancer
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the specific type and stage of cancer
diseases.medicine: and may include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy

diseases.name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism
diseases.medicine: management of complications, hormone replacement therapy (if necessary)

diseases.name: Goiter
symptoms: Enlarged thyroid gland in the neck
diseases.curing: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (medication
diseases.medicine: radioactive iodine therapy, surgery)

diseases.name: Urge Incontinence
symptoms: intense urge to urinate followed by involuntary urine leakage
diseases.medicine: pelvic floor exercises), medications (anticholinergics, beta-3 agonists), medical devices

diseases.name: Edward Syndrome
diseases.medicine: addressing associated medical issues, early intervention programs, palliative care

diseases.name: Muscular Dystrophy
diseases.medicine: physical therapy, assistive devices, medications for symptom management, gene therapies (in some cases)

diseases.name: Mittelschmerz
symptoms: Lower abdominal pain and discomfort occurring around the time of ovulation
diseases.medicine: heat therapy), hormonal birth control (if severe or recurrent)

diseases.name: Corneal Abrasion
diseases.medicine: lubricating eye drops, antibiotic ointments (if infected), avoiding further eye irritation

diseases.name: Anemia of Chronic Disease
diseases.medicine: addressing nutritional deficiencies, blood transfusions (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Dysthymic Disorder
symptoms: lasting for at least two years
diseases.medicine: interpersonal therapy), antidepressant medications

diseases.name: Scarlet Fever
diseases.medicine: rest, fluids, symptomatic relief

diseases.name: Hypertensive Heart Disease
diseases.medicine: lifestyle changes (healthy diet, exercise, stress management), treatment of underlying conditions

diseases.name: Drug Abuse (Barbiturates)
diseases.medicine: supportive care, counseling, rehabilitation programs

diseases.name: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome
diseases.medicine: exercise, weight management), hormonal contraceptives, fertility treatments (if needed)

diseases.name: Encephalitis
diseases.medicine: antiviral medications (if caused by a viral infection), supportive care

diseases.name: Cyst of the Eyelid
symptoms: Painful lump or swelling on the eyelid
diseases.medicine: good eyelid hygiene, antibiotic ointments (if infected), surgical removal (if necessary)

diseases.name: Balanitis
symptoms: Inflammation of the glans penis (tip of the penis)
diseases.medicine: topical antifungal or antibacterial creams, corticosteroid creams (if severe), treating underlying causes

diseases.name: Foreign Body in the Throat
symptoms: Sensation of a foreign object stuck in the throat
diseases.medicine: removal of the foreign body, evaluation for any complications

diseases.name: Drug Abuse (Cocaine)
symptoms: risk of heart attack or stroke
diseases.medicine: counseling, behavioral therapies, support groups

diseases.name: Optic Neuritis
diseases.medicine: treatment of underlying cause, symptom management, visual rehabilitation

diseases.name: Intestinal Malabsorption
diseases.curing: Dietary modifications (specific to the underlying cause)
diseases.medicine: supplementation of deficient nutrients, medications (to manage symptoms or underlying condition), treatment of underlying cause

diseases.name: Lead Poisoning
diseases.medicine: chelation therapy (in severe cases), supportive care, medication to manage symptoms, addressing complications (such as anemia)

diseases.name: Viral Warts
symptoms: rough growths on the skin or mucous membranes
may appear in clusters or as single lesions
diseases.medicine: cryotherapy), laser therapy, surgical removal, immune therapy (for persistent or recurrent cases)

diseases.name: Hyperhidrosis
symptoms: Excessive sweating (beyond what is necessary for temperature regulation)
typically in specific areas (such as palms
diseases.medicine: medications (anticholinergics, beta blockers), iontophoresis, Botox injections, surgical interventions (sympathectomy, sweat gland removal)

diseases.name: Stroke
symptoms: Sudden numbness or weakness of the face
or leg (typically on one side of the body)
diseases.medicine: medication (clot-busting drugs, anticoagulants), supportive care, rehabilitation (physical therapy, speech therapy), lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise, smoking cessation), addressing underlying risk factors

diseases.name: Pilonidal Cyst
symptoms: Pain or discomfort in the tailbone area
diseases.medicine: pain management, antibiotics (for infection), incision and drainage, surgical removal or excision, wound care, lifestyle modifications to prevent recurrence

diseases.name: Crushing Injury
symptoms: difficulty moving or using the affected area
diseases.medicine: immobilization, pain management, surgery (to repair or reconstruct affected tissues), rehabilitation (physical therapy, occupational therapy)

diseases.name: Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus
diseases.medicine: medication management for symptoms

diseases.name: Alopecia
diseases.medicine: oral medications (finasteride), steroid injections, hair transplant surgery, wigs or hairpieces

diseases.name: Hashimoto Thyroiditis
diseases.medicine: monitoring and management of symptoms

diseases.name: Flat Feet
symptoms: difficulty with foot and ankle movement
diseases.medicine: orthotic inserts, physical therapy exercises, pain management, surgery (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Nonalcoholic Liver Disease (NASH)

diseases.name: Pelvic Organ Prolapse
diseases.medicine: pessary use, hormone therapy, surgical repair (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Fracture of the Arm
symptoms: limited mobility or difficulty moving the arm
diseases.medicine: pain management, physical therapy, surgery (in complex fractures)

diseases.name: Coagulation (Bleeding) Disorder
symptoms: prolonged bleeding after injury or surgery
excessive bleeding from minor cuts or abrasions
diseases.medicine: clotting factor replacement therapy, medications to stimulate clotting, lifestyle modifications, surgery (in some cases)

diseases.name: Intracranial Hemorrhage
diseases.medicine: surgery (if needed), medications to control bleeding and manage symptoms

diseases.name: Hyperkalemia
diseases.medicine: medications to reduce potassium levels, dietary modifications, dialysis (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Cornea Infection
diseases.medicine: oral medications, supportive care, possible corneal transplant (in severe cases)

diseases.name: Abscess of the Lung
diseases.medicine: drainage or surgical removal of the abscess, supportive care, management of underlying lung conditions

diseases.name: Dengue Fever

diseases.name: Chronic Sinusitis

diseases.name: Cholesteatoma
diseases.medicine: antibiotics (for associated infections)

diseases.name: Volvulus

diseases.medicine: Emergency surgery to untwist the affected organ and restore blood flow

diseases.name: Injury to the Finger
symptoms: difficulty gripping or using the finger

diseases.name: Atrial Fibrillation

diseases.name: Pinworm Infection
symptoms: Itching around the anus or vagina

diseases.name: Urethral Valves
diseases.medicine: catheterization, medications for symptoms and complications

diseases.name: Migraine
diseases.medicine: lifestyle changes, preventive medications

diseases.name: Arthritis

diseases.name: Chronic Headache
diseases.medicine: relaxation techniques, stress management

diseases.name: Dry Skin
diseases.medicine: avoiding hot showers or baths, using mild soaps

diseases.name: Eczema
diseases.medicine: topical corticosteroids or immunomodulators, avoiding triggers

diseases.name: Psoriasis

diseases.name: Scabies
diseases.medicine: washing clothes and bedding in hot water, vacuuming and cleaning home

diseases.name: Urticaria (Hives)
diseases.medicine: avoiding triggers, corticosteroids (in severe cases)

diseases.medicine: oral antihistamines, cold compresses

diseases.name: Contact Dermatitis
symptoms: red rash caused by contact with irritants or allergens
diseases.medicine: corticosteroid creams or ointments, antihistamines

diseases.medicine: fluids, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief, antiviral medications (in some cases)

diseases.name: Bronchitis
symptoms: Persistent cough with yellow or green mucus
diseases.medicine: fluids, over-the-counter cough suppressants, inhalers (if prescribed), treating underlying cause

diseases.name: Gastroenteritis (Stomach Flu)
diseases.medicine: fluids (electrolyte solutions), bland diet, avoiding irritating foods, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief

diseases.name: Urinary Stones (Kidney Stones)
diseases.medicine: increased fluid intake, medications to help pass the stones, procedures or surgery

diseases.name: Osteoporosis

diseases.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis

diseases.medicine: Blood sugar monitoring, Healthy eating, Regular exercise
